hero.title: 7 Days in Ladakh.
hero.titleAccent: Monasteries, Pangong Lake and the Roof of the World.
hero.intro: Ladakh is like nowhere else in India. A high-altitude desert of stark mountains, turquoise lakes and centuries-old Buddhist monasteries, reached by flying into Leh. Seven days on the roof of the world.

breadcrumb.label: 7 Days Ladakh: Land of High Passes

hero.heroImageAlt: Ladakh Leh monasteries Pangong Lake high passes

overview.heading: 7 Days Ladakh: Land of High Passes: The Full Picture
body: Ladakh, the land of high passes, is a high-altitude desert in the far north of India, a place of stark, breathtaking mountains, turquoise lakes and ancient Buddhist monasteries. Flying into Leh, you enter a world of prayer flags, gompas clinging to cliffs, and some of the most dramatic landscapes on the planet.

This 7 day Ladakh package from Malaysia is arranged privately, with acclimatisation built in given the altitude. Your own vehicle and experienced driver guide take you to the great monasteries of the Indus valley, the Nubra Valley with its dunes and camels, and the unforgettable Pangong Lake, with permits arranged on your behalf.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Pangong Lake
items.body: The astonishing high-altitude lake that shifts through shades of blue and turquoise, set against barren mountains at over 4,300 metres. One of the most beautiful sights in the Himalayas.

items.title: Ladakh's Monasteries
items.body: Ancient Buddhist gompas like Thiksey, Hemis and Diskit cling to the mountains, full of prayer flags, murals and chanting monks, the spiritual heart of Ladakh.

items.title: Nubra Valley & Khardung La
items.body: Cross one of the world's highest motorable passes to reach the Nubra Valley, where sand dunes and double-humped Bactrian camels sit beneath snow peaks.

days.title: Fly to Leh
days.description: Fly into Leh and transfer to your hotel. A full rest day to acclimatise to the altitude.

days.title: Leh Local Sightseeing
days.description: Gentle local sights to continue acclimatising.

days.title: Indus Valley Monasteries
days.description: The great monasteries of the Indus valley.

days.title: Leh to Nubra Valley
days.description: Cross Khardung La to the Nubra Valley.

days.title: Nubra to Pangong Lake
days.description: Drive across to the spectacular Pangong Lake.

days.title: Pangong to Leh
days.description: Sunrise at the lake, then return to Leh over Chang La.

days.title: Depart Leh
days.description: Transfer to Leh airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Ladakh, Done Safely at Altitude
whyBook.body: Ladakh's extreme altitude and remote roads demand experience. We build in acclimatisation, use seasoned mountain drivers and arrange permits. Dhesu has arranged Himalayan journeys for many years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Ladakh Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
Ladakh is one of the most extraordinary destinations in India, a high-altitude desert of stark mountains, turquoise lakes and ancient monasteries. This 7 day package, flying into Leh, covers the highlights with acclimatisation built in.

You visit the great Indus valley monasteries, cross Khardung La to the Nubra Valley, and reach the unforgettable Pangong Lake, all with permits arranged and an experienced driver guide.

For an overland version, see our 12 day Srinagar to Leh road trip.

sections.heading: Altitude and the Best Time to Go
sections.body:
Leh sits at around 3,500 metres, and the passes are far higher, so altitude sickness is a real consideration. This itinerary includes a full acclimatisation day on arrival, and we strongly advise taking the altitude seriously.

Ladakh is best visited from June to September, when the weather is mild and the high passes and lakes are accessible. Flights into Leh operate year-round, but the full circuit needs the summer months.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (IXL). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: How serious is the altitude in Ladakh?
items.answer: Very. Leh sits around 3,500 metres and the passes are much higher. Altitude sickness is a real risk, so this itinerary includes a full rest day on arrival. We advise consulting your doctor before travelling and taking it easy on the first days.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Ladakh?
items.answer: June to September is ideal, with mild weather and the high passes and lakes accessible. Outside these months, snow closes the passes and conditions are extreme. Flights into Leh operate year-round, but the full circuit needs summer.

items.question: Do I need permits?
items.answer: Yes. Inner Line Permits are required for areas like Nubra Valley and Pangong Lake. We assist with arranging these as part of the package. You will need passport copies and photos, which we will request before your trip.
